title: See the delicate crescent moon dance with the 'Seven Sisters' of the Pleiades tonight
summary: The Pleiades star cluster, also known as the 'Seven Sisters,' will be visible near the crescent moon tonight. This open star cluster in the constellation Taurus consists of around 1,000 stars.

title: What if the next great astronomer isn't human? How AI is revolutionizing our study of the cosmos
summary: AI is transforming astronomy by enabling new discoveries and collaborations with human astronomers. This innovative partnership is unlocking previously unseen potential in cosmic research.

title: 'This is really intolerable': Astronomers protest giant orbiting mirror project and SpaceX's million AI satellites
summary: Astronomers are protesting a proposed project involving giant orbiting mirrors and SpaceX's planned million AI satellites, warning it would severely disrupt the night sky. Experts argue these constellations would destroy humanity's view of the stars as we've known it for millennia.

title: Watch Russia launch Progress 94 cargo ship to the ISS on March 22
summary: Russia is set to launch the Progress 94 cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station on March 22. The event can be watched live.

title: We chat to legendary space shuttle commander Eileen Collins about her new documentary, 'Spacewoman' (interview)
summary: A new documentary titled 'Spacewoman' highlights the achievements of astronaut Eileen Collins. The interview discusses her career as a legendary space shuttle commander.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 202 — Artemis Imminent
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' discusses the imminent Artemis missions with Mike Wall from Space.com. The podcast covers recent updates and developments related to NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis Accords nations grapple with how to handle emergencies and 'harmful interference' on the moon
summary: Nations involved in the Artemis Accords are addressing challenges around emergency responses and preventing harmful interference during lunar missions as moon exploration intensifies. The discussions aim to establish guidelines for managing competing operations on the moon.

title: The loneliest places in the universe might actually be some of the best places for life
summary: Extreme exomoons with tidal heating and hydrogen-rich atmospheres could sustain billions of years of habitable conditions, challenging the idea that isolated places in the universe are lifeless.

title: Would you like to know more about 'Starship Troopers: Ultimate Bug War!'? Developer Auroch Digital has the answers (interview)
summary: Developer Auroch Digital discusses the latest Starship Troopers video game, highlighting its unique features and strengths compared to previous installments.

title: Astronomers keep finding new moons of Jupiter and Saturn
summary: Astronomers have discovered four new moons orbiting Jupiter and 11 new moons around Saturn, expanding the known celestial bodies in our solar system.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second flight, marking the start of numerous test flights planned for 2026. The abbreviated flight due to a technical issue still allowed the team to gather valuable data before a safe landing.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: Restoration of Hangar One, a historic landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area and a key part of the region's early aviation history, has been completed. The project was finished by Planetary Ventures in December 2025.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging students to design innovative concepts for human exploration on the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA has collected extensive data on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, which is leaving our solar system, and will make this information publicly available. This marks the third identified interstellar object to visit our solar system.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes spacesuit fit verification inside the ISS's Quest airlock to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure also involves fellow astronaut Jessica Meir to assess safety and comfort.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission launch is set to inspire the upcoming film 'Project Hail Mary'. The real-life space exploration and the fictional movie will both explore deep space journeys.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its test flight around the Moon. The simulations helped ensure the best possible launch conditions by analyzing the rocket's flight environment.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, offering hands-on learning for high school juniors and seniors to prepare for aerospace careers. Applications are open from March 20 to May 1.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano in Hawaii entered its 43rd eruption episode, producing high lava fountains and widespread ash dispersal. This marks another significant event in the ongoing volcanic activity.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida conducted annual bald eagle nesting surveys to document active and inactive nests. An American bald eagle was observed flying away from its nest on March 13, 2026.

title: Progress MS-33 set to resume Russian flights to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 is scheduled to resume Russian flights to the International Space Station (ISS) from a repaired launch pad. This follows a nearly four-month gap after the Soyuz MS-28 launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the manufacturing pace of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company aims to expand its space industry capabilities with these initiatives.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This development brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, including missions by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum. The launches highlight a busy period in the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth. The mission concept was unveiled as part of their latest space industry initiatives.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Mars missions at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to Starship development. The company's ambitious goals are highlighted as it scales up manufacturing for deep-space exploration.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) saw new crew arrivals and highlighted scientific activities in February after Crew-11 departed early due to a medical concern in mid-January.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. The Falcon 9 is expected to continue its dominance in commercial and government missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since Flight 11 on October 13, 2025. This development follows a 147-day gap since the last major flight.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 hallmark achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the recovery, marking a significant milestone for the mission.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The spacecraft is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket by January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will release ECOS version 6 software on 30 March 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky and expensive solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for research. This initiative aims to support educational and scientific advancements in space technology.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new business called Satcat Terminal. This development is expected to benefit liberal arts majors.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity. The country's space ecosystem is described as small but significant.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service to make it operational rather than experimental. The initiative aims to maintain critical satellite infrastructure for communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster margins. The launch is now expected to occur this summer.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The funding will support their efforts to develop alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for launch activities.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry is experiencing rapid growth, but the supply chain is struggling to keep up. Industry groups suggest that policymakers can accelerate progress.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after restructuring in 2025, including divesting its maritime business and reducing debt. CEO Theresa Condor outlined the ambitious target to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's transformation, OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand during his first year in the role.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions through a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris with regular, commercialized solutions.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI is set to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The delivery marks a significant step in modernizing the Army's communication systems.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump’s Cyber Strategy for America, focusing on securing critical infrastructure and maintaining leadership in emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum computing.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: The UK is discussing strategies to enhance the security of its space assets and strategic stability in space, as highlighted in a panel at Space-Comm in London. The discussion involved perspectives from both government and industry leaders.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. This contract highlights SpiderOak's role in enhancing security and reliability in military drone systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the U.K. discussed the future of warfare during an event at Chatham House, marking four years since Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted evolving military strategies and challenges.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it will not cause interference. The company argues that the SkyTerra Next proposal aligns with existing L-band coordination.
